nuggets.headline: Radiohead's producer Nigel Godrich started as an engineer on *The Bends*
nuggets.text: Before becoming Radiohead's long-time producer, Nigel Godrich was initially hired as an engineer for their 1995 album, *The Bends*. The band, already approving of his efforts to take their sound in new directions, nicknamed him "Nihilist." This early collaboration evolved into a partnership where Godrich would go on to produce every Radiohead album from *OK Computer* onwards.

source.title: Radiohead producer, Nigel Godrich debuts Ultraista LP.
source.quoteSnippet: Godrich first worked with the rock band Radiohead when John Leckie hired him at RAK to engineer their EP My Iron Lung (1994) and their second album, The Bends (1995). The band nicknamed him "Nihilist", approving of his efforts to take their sound in new directions. ... Radiohead invited Godrich to co-produce their third album, OK Computer (1997). ... He has produced all of Radiohead's albums since...

nuggets.imageCaption: Nigel Godrich, long-time producer for Radiohead and other iconic artists.

nuggets.headline: Radiohead's "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi" features an arpeggiated melody musicians love to explore
nuggets.text: The intricate, arpeggiated guitar melody central to "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi" holds a special appeal for musicians. Many describe it as a "hypnotic raga," a loop that's compelling to play around with for extended periods, easily drawing a musician into its depths. This technical aspect of the song offers a unique insight into its creation and enduring appeal.

source.title: 19 Thoughts on Radiohead's In Rainbows
source.quoteSnippet: The arpeggiated melody that anchors the song sounds like a real pleasant loop for a musician to play around with for hours on end—a hypnotic raga of sorts that one can get lost in forever if they’re not careful.

nuggets.imageCaption: The arpeggiated guitar line is a captivating element for musicians.

nuggets.headline: explore Atoms for Peace, featuring Radiohead's Thom Yorke and drummer Joey Waronker
nuggets.text: Atoms for Peace presents a fascinating extension of Radiohead's rhythmic explorations. This supergroup features Thom Yorke and Nigel Godrich alongside acclaimed drummer Joey Waronker, known for his work with Beck and R.E.M. Waronker's unique ability to translate complicated electronic rhythms into live instrumentation was central to the band's sound, creating a blend of electronic and acoustic elements. Start with their debut album, *Amok*, to hear this fusion in action.

source.title: A New Career in a New Town: Thom Yorke and Nigel Godrich run AMOK as Atoms for Peace - Fact Magazine
source.quoteSnippet: Atoms for Peace– a collaboration between Radiohead frontman Thom Yorke and the band’s producer Nigel Godrich – release their debut album AMOK. Enlisting musicians Flea (Red Hot Chili Peppers, The Mars Volta), Joey Waronker (drummer for Beck and later REM) and Mauro Refosco (percussionist for David Byrne and others), Atoms for Peace formed as an attempt to recreate music from Yorke’s 2006 solo album The Eraser in a live setting, but swiftly morphed into something different.

nuggets.imageCaption: Atoms for Peace, featuring Thom Yorke, Nigel Godrich, and Joey Waronker.

artistSummary: Radiohead consistently pushes boundaries, earning a reputation as one of the most influential bands of their generation. Their albums are often characterized by innovative production and evolving musical styles, creating a distinctive and impactful sound that continues to inspire.
